Notice Of Motion For Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Including Atty Fees.
Off Calendar Jan-23-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Harold Auerbach, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: George Ballard Company

NO OTHER APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff George Ballard's motion for an order to deem certain matters admitted and to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. The matters set forth in plaintiff's requests for admissions are ordered admitted. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections within 21 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $315 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Be Relieved As Counsel

Notice And Motion For Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Facts Be Admitted And Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And For Sanctions Including Attys' Fees
Off Calendar Nov-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff George Ballard Company's motion to deem certain matters admitted and to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. The motion was filed on October 19, 2007. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-07-2007 Set For Trial On Feb-11-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jan-30-08 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 503
